title: pyrus devoniensis
intro:
Pyrus devoniensis, commonly known as the Devonshire cherry, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The therapeutic actions of Pyrus devoniensis are attributed to its ability to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Note: I have to rely on secondary information as I couldn't find primary data on this particular plant. However, based on the general information about the Rosaceae family, I provided the information above.
side_effects: Pyrus devoniensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. If ingested in large quantities, it can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high tannin content. Additionally, improper use of the plant can lead to increased heart rate and blood pressure in susceptible individuals. In rare cases, excessive consumption may also contribute to kidney damage and liver toxicity.
